adoption_track.title: 10-Minute Quickstart
adoption_track.purpose: A runnable, copy-paste quickstart that gets a developer from clone to first API call in 10 minutes.

adoption_track.title: Minimal Python SDK
adoption_track.purpose: A minimal Python client/SDK that wraps the OpenAPI contract with typed requests and responses.

adoption_track.title: LangGraph/LangChain Integration Example
adoption_track.purpose: A concrete example showing how GrantLayer grant requests, evidence bundles, and policy checks fit into a LangGraph node graph.

adoption_track.title: Public GitHub Readiness Pack
adoption_track.purpose: A checklist and set of artifacts (README, CONTRIBUTING, issue templates, CI badges) that prepare the repo for public visibility.

adoption_track.title: First Developer Feedback Log
adoption_track.purpose: A structured log or template for capturing the first real (or explicitly simulated) developer feedback on the quickstart, SDK, and integration example.

risks.mitigation: Messaging rules and explicit non-goal statements in every adoption issue.
Validation gates require examples to be tested; quickstart must be copy-paste runnable.
Validation gates require no OpenAPI drift unless explicitly planned; SDK must track OpenAPI.
Quickstart must work on a clean clone with no hidden config; document all env vars.
Clearly distinguish demo-mode from product-mode; never present demo-mode as secure.
Automated scan for PEM markers, tokens, and passwords in all docs and examples.
Minimal Python SDK must use only stdlib + cryptography (same as backend); document install steps.
Every example must have a corresponding validation test that runs in CI.

validation_gates: Docs are copy-paste runnable where applicable
No OpenAPI drift unless explicitly planned
Full backend suite on main before push
non_goals: No public GitHub release in GL-145
